D6 Display L5 Evaluate Reference

ColorSync: Managed vs Not

a tagged image only looks right if something converts its numbers from the source profile to the display's — that something is the OS colour engine

An image file stores numbers plus an ICC profile saying what those numbers mean. macOS ColorSync reads the source profile, converts through a profile connection space, and re-encodes for the display's own profile. Skip that step — show the raw numbers as if they were sRGB — and a wide-gamut image renders over-saturated and wrong.

Choose a source profile and toggle colour management. Managed, the swatch matches its intended colour; unmanaged, the same stored numbers are misread as sRGB and the colour shifts. Wider source gamuts shift more.
